notes: Eleventh validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its public-facing identity calls the school the Home of the Mavericks.
Its mission says the school supports families by providing the best educational experience possible in a moral and wholesome environment.
Official materials say the school is guided by R.A.I.S.E. values: Respect, Accountability, Integrity, Service, and Excellence.
The school says leadership and character development are woven into daily life through leadership lessons, collaborative projects, and service opportunities.
Its board page says regular board meetings are held virtually on the third Thursday of the month at 1:00 p.m.
Official pages list Dr. Bridget Jones as Director and Kyle Shrauger as Board Chair.
Its public-facing identity calls the school the Home of the Warriors.
Its mission says the school supports families by offering the best educational experience possible in a moral and wholesome environment.
The school says it is guided by R.A.I.S.E. values and aims to develop capable, compassionate leaders who make a positive impact in their communities.
Official pages describe the school as formerly operated as Aristotle Preparatory Academy.
Its board page says board meetings are held virtually on the third Thursday of the month at 1:00 p.m.
Official pages list Carmi Green as Director and Kyle Shrauger as Board Chair.
Its mission says the school supports families by offering the best educational experience possible in a moral and wholesome environment.
Official materials say the school is guided by R.A.I.S.E. values: Respect, Accountability, Integrity, Service, and Excellence.
The school says students are inspired to Learn. Lead. Change the World!
The school's public materials highlight Glenn Way as founder and feature his statement about helping students become the best spouses, parents, citizens, and leaders.
Its board page says regular board meetings are held virtually on the fourth Monday of the month at 7:00 p.m.
Official pages list Talia Wright as Director and Matt Waring as Chairman of the Board.
Its public-facing identity calls the school the Home of the Eagles.
Its mission says the school cultivates both intellectual and personal character, preparing students to excel in college and future careers while emerging as positive leaders in their communities.
Official materials say the school extends a classical education model and emphasizes leadership development and real-world readiness.
The school says it teaches through Core Knowledge, classical practices, and the R.A.I.S.E. virtues.
Its board page says regular board meetings are held on the third Tuesday of each month at 4:45 p.m.
Official pages list Christy Williamsen and Ramon Richardson as school leaders, with Rev. Martin McCarthy serving as board chair.
Its signature statement is "The World is Our Family."
Its mission says the school nurtures students' natural curiosity and joy of learning through a challenging, interdisciplinary, international curriculum.
Official materials say the school aims to develop critical and compassionate thinkers, confident and open-minded communicators, and resourceful and responsible citizens and leaders.
Its four core values are Respect, Responsibility, Solutions, and Service.
The school says its curriculum includes international and environmental studies and that athletics compete in both NCHSAA and the Carolina Beltway Conference.
Official pages list Dr. Paul Bryant as Head of School and Mrs. La-Kisha Donaldson as Principal.
